svg.text: Modern OoO LSU + L1D Baseline Pipeline
Generic RISC-V-friendly baseline for interview reasoning. Queues are storage; rectangular boxes are pipeline stages.
Fast L1D-hit timing: C0 issue -> C1 AGU/DTLB/index -> C2 tag/data + SQ check -> C3 align/wakeup -> C4 dependent use. Dispatch and commit are outside load-to-use.
used for violation search and commit
predict: load may issue or must wait
selective replay or flush from load
lets ROB retire before cache accepts store
holds paddr, data, byte mask, coherence state
drains when L1D port/ownership is available
bank conflict and write-port conflict source
see Part 6 for detailed coherence model
Memory uops allocate ordering state before they execute out of order.
dispatch -> C0 register: mem_uop, lq_idx/sq_idx, src tags, rob_idx, branch mask
C0: memory issue, register read, and bypass
Loads and store-address uops need base register. Store-data uops may arrive independently.
C0 -> C1 register: base/data operands, lq/sq index, op size, sign/zero extend, memory ordering metadata
C1: AGU, DTLB, and L1D index/bank generation
Generate virtual address, translate to physical address, and carry precise fault information with the uop.
C1 -> C2 register: physical address, bank, byte mask, fault bits, LQ/SQ age metadata
C2: SQ ordering/forwarding check in parallel with L1D tag/data
The hardest LSU timing path is often address compare, age filtering, data select, and replay decision.
C2 -> C3 register: forwarding decision, replay cause, paddr/bank, store-hit mask, selected store bytes
C2/C3: L1D banked access, MSHR, and miss handling
Loads access tag/data; committed stores and refills arbitrate for write/update resources.
C3 -> C4 register: cache data, hit/miss, refill/forward bytes, replay status, exception bits
C3/C4: load data merge, bypass/wakeup, and commit/store drain
Load result becomes architectural only when the ROB later commits; store visibility waits for commit.
ROB commit marks SQ entry safe
AGU -> DTLB/L1D index -> SQ CAM/age filter -> forward/merge MUX -> WB/wakeup
store address resolve -> LQ CAM violation search -> selective replay or flush decision
LQ/SQ full, unknown-store stalls, forwarding hits/partial hits, violation replays, bank conflicts, MSHR full, cache/TLB misses, store-buffer full, writeback-port conflicts.
Do not count these as one additive CPI bucket when events overlap; keep diagnostic counters and explain the dominant bottleneck.
